description: MakeSense.ai evaluation suite — classification, extraction, and insight quality across genres, sizes, and edge cases. Inspired by Checklist (Ribeiro et al.), LMUnit natural-language unit tests, and real-world expense/todo NLP patterns.

categories.mixed: Multiple content types in one note

cases.name: Clean comma-separated list
cases.input: Swiggy 450, petrol 1200, Netflix 649

cases.name: Prose-style single expense
cases.input: Paid 100 for petrol at Indian Oil on the way to office.

cases.name: Multi-line receipt-style
cases.input:
Amazon - wireless mouse Rs 899
DMart groceries 2340
Uber to airport 650

cases.name: Explicit USD currency
cases.input: AWS bill $47.20, GitHub Pro $4/mo, domain renewal $12

checks.note: Dates may be ambiguous DD/MM vs MM/DD — should flag or pick consistently

cases.name: Relative date yesterday
cases.input: Yesterday: dinner at Social 1800, movie tickets 600

cases.name: Large expense list (10 items)
cases.input:
Mon coffee 120
Tue lunch 280
Wed metro 80
Thu dinner 650
Fri snacks 90
Sat groceries 1100
Sun brunch 450
Netflix 649
Spotify 119
Gym 2000

cases.name: Income mixed with expense
cases.input: Got salary 85000. Paid rent 25000 and electricity 3200.

checks.note: Should extract expenses; income handling is out of scope for v0

cases.name: Hinglish expenses
cases.input: Petrol ke liye 300 pay kiya, Swiggy order 450 UPI se, chai 30 rupees

cases.name: Hindi numerals mixed
cases.input: 500 रुपये का पेट्रोल भराया और 200 का samosa

checks.note: Devanagari numerals are a hard edge case

cases.name: Bullet list
cases.input:
- Call dentist
- Buy milk
- Finish report by Friday

cases.name: Numbered list with priorities
cases.input:
1. URGENT fix prod bug
2. Review PR from Alex
3. Low priority: organize desk

cases.name: Relative dates
cases.input:
tomorrow: submit taxes
next week: team offsite prep
urgent: fix prod bug

cases.name: Completed tasks marked
cases.input:
Done: buy groceries
[x] send invoice
Still need to call landlord about lease

cases.name: Task dependencies
cases.input: First get approval from manager, then submit expense report, after that book travel

checks.note: Should capture depends_on chain if possible

cases.name: Prose-style errands
cases.input: Need to pick up dry cleaning, remember to water the plants, and maybe look into car insurance quotes this weekend.

cases.name: Personal journal
cases.input: Today felt overwhelming. The meeting went okay but I'm not sure about the roadmap. Maybe I need a break this weekend.

cases.name: Meeting notes
cases.input: Q2 planning - Sarah owns API migration, deadline March 15. Budget approved at 2M. Risk: vendor delay on auth module.

checks.note: Ideal future type: meeting_notes with owners/deadlines

cases.name: Research brainstorm
cases.input: Ideas for the blog post: compare LangGraph vs custom DAG orchestrators, mention AOP paper from Tsinghua, include cost routing table for small vs large models.

cases.name: Short thought fragment
cases.input: what if we segmented notes before classifying?

cases.name: Expenses + todos in one note
cases.input: Spent 500 on lunch at Olive. Also need to call mom and book flight to Delhi for next month.

checks.note: v0 picks dominant type only — should ideally detect BOTH blocks

cases.name: Shopping list that looks like expenses
cases.input: Buy: milk 60, bread 40, eggs 90. Total budget 200.

checks.note: Planned spending vs actual — ambiguous intent

cases.name: Trip planning mashup
cases.input: Goa trip Dec 20-25. Book flights (~8000), hotel 15000/night x 4. TODO: get leave approved, pack sunscreen, tell Rohan.

checks.note: Rich multi-block content — stress test for segmentation

cases.name: Tiny input (3 words)
cases.input: buy milk tomorrow

cases.name: Medium journal (~150 words)
cases.input: I've been thinking about how we classify user notes. The current approach sends everything to a single classifier, which works for clean inputs but breaks down when someone mixes a grocery list with their diary entry. Maybe we need a segmentation pass first — split the note into paragraphs or bullet groups, classify each chunk independently, then merge results. That would match what the product plan describes but isn't implemented yet. For now the classifier picks the dominant type, which means mixed notes lose information. I should test this with real inputs from friends who actually use notepads casually.

cases.name: Negation — not a todo
cases.input: Don't need to buy milk. Already have enough.

checks.note: Should NOT extract 'buy milk' as active task

cases.name: Sarcastic expense
cases.input: Great, another 500 rupees down the drain on 'productivity' apps I'll never open.

checks.note: Should extract 500 even with sarcastic tone

cases.name: Code snippet in note
Question-only input
cases.input: How much did I spend on food last month?

checks.note: Query about data, not a note — should summarize not invent expenses
